metadata.scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship for the Students Belonging to Scheduled Tribe for Studies in India

raw_data.details: The scheme "Post-Matric Scholarship for the Students Belonging to Scheduled Tribe for Studies in India" is implemented by the Department of Tribal Affairs (Plain), Government of Assam. The scheme aims to enable eligible Scheduled Tribe students to undertake quality education from the post-matriculation to the post-graduate level by providing them with comprehensive financial assistance. The scheme provides financial assistance covering the payment of compulsory non-refundable fees and a monthly stipend, alongside an additional disability allowance for specially-abled students.
raw_data.benefits: Fee ComponentThe scheme covers the payment of compulsory non-refundable fees for the recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary course pursued by the student. There is a fee ceiling of ₹2,50,000 per annum per student for Engineering courses in private sector institutes. There is a fee ceiling of ₹6,00,000 per annum per student for Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery, Master of Surgery, and Doctor of Medicine courses in private sector institutes. There is a fee ceiling of ₹1,00,000 per annum per student for all other courses in private sector institutes. Monthly Stipend (Payable For A Maximum Of 10 Months Annually)For Group I (Degree and Post Graduate professional courses): Hostellers receive ₹1,200 per month (₹12,000 annually), and day scholars receive ₹550 per month (₹5,500 annually). For Group II (Non-professional Degree and Post Graduate courses): Hostellers receive ₹820 per month (₹8,200 annually), and day scholars receive ₹530 per month (₹5,300 annually). For Group III (Vocational, Industrial Training Institute, and 3-year diploma courses): Hostellers receive ₹570 per month (₹5,700 annually), and day scholars receive ₹300 per month (₹3,000 annually). For Group IV (Post-matriculation non-degree courses): Hostellers receive ₹380 per month (₹3,800 annually), and day scholars receive ₹230 per month (₹2,300 annually). Additional Disability AllowanceSpecially-abled hosteller students receive an additional allowance of ₹800 per month (₹9,600 annually). Specially-abled day scholar students receive an additional allowance of ₹600 per month (₹7,200 annually). Benefit Disbursal DetailsThe scholarship amounts are disbursed exclusively through the Direct Benefit Transfer mode directly into the Aadhaar-linked bank accounts of the beneficiaries. The stipend is payable monthly, calculated from 1st April or from the month of admission, whichever is later, until the month in which the annual examinations are completed. Conditions for Availment and ContinuationThe continuous disbursement of the scholarship is strictly dependent on the satisfactory academic progress, good conduct, and regularity in attendance of the enrolled scholars. The scholarship will be immediately cancelled or withheld if the student resorts to strikes or maintains irregular attendance without formal permission from the institution authorities. The scholarship will not be renewed if the student fails in a particular academic year, and the benefits will be paused until the student clears that class and gets promoted to the next class.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe, Other Backward Classes, or More Other Backward Classes as specified in relation to the State or Union Territory to which they actually belong. The applicant must have successfully passed the Matriculation or Higher Secondary or any higher examination of a recognized University or Board of Secondary Education. The applicant must take admission in recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary courses pursued in government, government-aided, or approved private institutions. The applicant must possess a valid, active bank account in a Scheduled Bank. The applicant must link their bank account securely with their Aadhaar number and their active mobile number. The applicant should preferably continue using the identical mobile number linked with the bank account for the entire duration of the course. Income CriteriaThe applicant must belong to a family whose total gross income from all sources does not exceed ₹2,50,000 per annum without any exemption or deductions. The applicant must calculate the combined income of both parents from all sources if both the father and mother are currently working. The applicant must compute the income of only one parent if only one parent is alive and supporting the family. The applicant must not include the income of siblings or other earning members of the family while computing the total family income. The applicant must be fully exempt from the family income criteria if they are an orphan supported by a guardian.
raw_data.exclusions: The applicant must not be in receipt of any other scholarship or stipend from any alternative scheme at the same time. The applicant must not apply for a diploma or degree course in a different academic stream after already completing a separate course in one stream. The applicant must not claim the scholarship stipend for the period of internship or housemanship if they receive any form of remuneration or allowance during that practical training period.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineSignup to DigiLocker
Step 1: Visit the [DigiLocker Portal](https://www.digilocker.gov.in/) or download the DigiLocker Mobile App ([Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.digilocker.android) / [iOS](https://itunes.apple.com/in/app/digilocker/id1320618078)).
Step 2: Click "[Login/Register](https://www.digilocker.gov.in/web/signup)" and provide these details: Full name, Gender, Mobile number, Email ID, and Aadhaar Number. Create a 6-digit PIN.
Step 3: Finally, click "Submit". Note the 6-digit PIN for future reference. Signin to myScheme
Step 1: At the top right corner of this page, click "Sign In". Provide your DigiLocker Mobile Number and the 6-digit PIN.
Step 2: Click the "I consent to terms of use" checkbox, then click "Sign In". You will be directed to the DigiLocker Consent Page.
Step 3: At the bottom of the page, click "Allow". You have now successfully signed in to myScheme. Scheme Application
Step 1: At the top of this page, click "Apply Now". The application form of the scheme will open.
Step 2: Fill in all the mandatory fields of the application form, and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
Step 3: Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections.
Step 4: Click "Submit". You'll receive the following confirmation message: "Form Submitted Successfully!".
Step 5: For future reference, click "Print" to print the form or to download the filled form as a PDF file. Else, click "Ok" to get redirected to the myScheme landing page.
raw_data.documents_required: During Application PhasePassport Size Photograph Of The Applicant. Aadhaar Card Of The Applicant. Domicile Certificate Of The Applicant. Caste Certificate Issued By The Competent Authority Of The State Or Union Territory. Family Income Certificate Issued By The Competent Authority (Sub-Deputy Collector, Block Development Officer, Mouzadar, Employer, Or Income Tax Officer). Income Declaration Form (Signed By The Parents, Guardian, Or Husband). Scanned Copy Of Certificates And Mark Sheets Of All Examinations Passed By The Applicant. Disability Certificate Issued By The Competent Medical Authority Of The State Government (Required Only For Specially-Abled Students). Certificate From The Hostel Superintendent (Required Only If Residing In An Approved Hostel). During Renewal PhaseMarksheet Or Result Certificate Of The Previous Academic Year Passed. Medical Certificate Endorsed By The Head Of The Institution (Required Only If The Applicant Was Unable To Appear In The Annual Examination Due To Illness).
raw_data.scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship for the Students Belonging to Scheduled Tribe for Studies in India
